summaries: The performance-gate fix measures cumulative monotonic elapsed time across exactly the 16 accepted production CLI invocations, matching the revised accepted-chain process budget.

Topology setup and negative-fixture preparation are excluded only from the latency metric; mandatory fail-closed negatives remain executable assertions and the complete suite proves 28/28 continuity cases.

The late npm PATH fix preserves verified package-manager self-resolution without changing canonical absolute command argv, closed environment construction, HOME isolation, or exact npm/go provenance checks.

Runner bounds, dual-repository mutation detection, pathname/TOCTOU limitations, signed review validation, approval-only commit topology, and formal artifact/receipt chain remain fail closed within the declared threat boundary.
No Critical or Important security or code-quality finding remains in the exact candidate.

Independent continuity evidence: 28/28 mandatory negative cases passed with checkpoint peak 6,275,445 bytes.
